question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRealm()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by submitting transactions whose entity IDs are constructed with a mismatched realm value, given that realm defaults to 0 and is used globally in EntityId construction, causing all records for non-zero realm entities to be silently dropped or misrouted by the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getShard()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by submitting transactions referencing entities in a shard other than the configured shard value, given that shard is used in all EntityId lookups, causing the mirror node to fail to match incoming records to stored entities and silently skip them?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a Spring context refresh (e.g., via a crafted actuator endpoint or config reload) that calls init() a second time, overwriting the INSTANCE AtomicReference with a new CommonProperties object with different realm/shard values, causing in-flight record processing to use inconsistent shard/realm and produce incorrect or missing exported records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that reaches getInstance() before the Spring context is fully initialized (e.g., via a race condition during startup), causing an IllegalStateException that aborts record processing and results in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getInstance()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static singleton pattern in getInstance() by triggering concurrent access during Spring context initialization, causing a null INSTANCE to be returned transiently and throwing IllegalStateException, which aborts the record import pipeline and causes missing records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRealm() / getShard()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by exploiting the absence of a @Max constraint on realm and shard, allowing these values to be set to Long.MAX_VALUE via environment variable injection, causing arithmetic overflow in EntityId.of(shard, realm, num) and producing incorrect entity IDs that mismatch all incoming records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the enabled=true default in DatabaseStartupProperties, where if an attacker can inject HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_DATABASE_STARTUP_ENABLED=false via a misconfigured environment, the mirror node starts without waiting for the database, connects to an unavailable DB, and silently drops all incoming records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.getTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the @DurationMin(seconds=10) minimum on timeout, where setting timeout to exactly 10 seconds in a slow environment causes the DatabaseWaiter to throw IllegalStateException before the DB is ready, aborting startup and causing all records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.getConnectTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the @DurationMin(seconds=1) minimum on connectTimeout, where setting connectTimeout to 1 second in a high-latency environment causes all DB connection attempts to time out, the DatabaseWaiter exhausts its total timeout, and the mirror node fails to start, resulting in no records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.getSocketTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the @DurationMin(seconds=1) minimum on socketTimeout, where setting socketTimeout to 1 second causes mid-transfer DB connections to be severed during large record batch writes, resulting in partial or missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.getValidationTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the @DurationMax(seconds=Integer.MAX_VALUE) upper bound on validationTimeout, where setting validationTimeout to Integer.MAX_VALUE seconds causes Connection.isValid() to block indefinitely, stalling the DatabaseWaiter and preventing the mirror node from ever starting, resulting in all records being missing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.getInterval()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the @DurationMin(millis=500) minimum on interval combined with @DurationUnit(ChronoUnit.SECONDS), where the unit annotation causes the value to be interpreted in seconds but validated in milliseconds, allowing interval to be set to 0 seconds (0ms, below the 500ms minimum), causing a tight retry loop that exhausts CPU and prevents record processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRealm()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by exploiting the fact that realm is used in SystemEntity.toEntityId() to construct address book entity IDs (101, 102), where a misconfigured realm causes the mirror node to look up the wrong address book, accept records from unauthorized nodes, and export incorrect consensus data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getShard()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by exploiting the fact that shard is used in SystemEntity.toEntityId() to construct the fee schedule file entity ID (111), where a misconfigured shard causes the mirror node to apply the wrong fee schedule when validating transactions, resulting in incorrect transaction records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRealm() / getShard()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by exploiting the use of CommonProperties.getInstance() in RecordItem.isSystemFileUpdate(), where a misconfigured realm/shard causes the system account check (EntityId.of(shard, realm, 50L)) to never match the actual payer, causing system file updates to be misclassified as non-top-level transactions and processed incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRealm() / getShard()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the use of CommonProperties.getInstance() in DomainUtils.fromEvmAddress(), where a misconfigured realm/shard causes long-zero EVM addresses to be resolved to wrong EntityIds, causing contract records to fail entity lookups and be silently dropped from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRealm() / getShard()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by exploiting the use of CommonProperties.getInstance() in NetworkProperties.createUnreleasedSupplyRangeBounds(), where a misconfigured realm/shard causes the unreleased supply account ranges to be computed with wrong EntityIds, resulting in incorrect token supply calculations being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the lack of idempotency protection in init(), where INSTANCE.set(this) unconditionally overwrites any previously set instance, allowing a second Spring bean initialization (e.g., via prototype scope or test context) to replace the singleton with a zero-realm/shard instance, causing all subsequent record processing to use wrong entity IDs?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.getConnectTimeout() / getSocketTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the fact that connectTimeout and socketTimeout are passed as JDBC properties only 'when supported', where an unsupported driver silently ignores these timeouts, causing the DatabaseWaiter to hang indefinitely on a slow connection and never complete startup, resulting in no records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRealm()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the fact that realm is used in EntityIdAliasParameter.valueOfNullable() to resolve alias-based entity IDs, where a misconfigured realm causes alias lookups to resolve to wrong entities, causing alias-based transaction records to fail database lookups and be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getShard()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the fact that shard is used in EntityIdEvmAddressParameter.valueOfNullable() to resolve EVM address-based entity IDs, where a misconfigured shard causes EVM address lookups to resolve to wrong entities, causing EVM-based transaction records to fail database lookups and be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: DatabaseStartupProperties.isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missing records by exploiting the boolean enabled flag in DatabaseStartupProperties, where setting enabled=false via Spring Boot environment variable causes the mirror node to skip database readiness checks entirely, connect to a not-yet-ready database, and fail to persist incoming records, resulting in missing exports?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/CommonProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRealm() / getShard()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by exploiting the fact that realm and shard have no cross-validation against the actual Hedera network configuration, where setting realm=1 on a shard=0 network causes all entity IDs to be constructed with wrong realm, causing the mirror node to export records with incorrect entity references?
